content.problemStatement:
Toofan wanted to write the string `"ADVITIYA"`, but accidentally wrote the 8-letter string SSS instead.   He wants to correct his mistake with the minimum number of steps.

In one step, Toofan can choose an index iii (1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N), and change SiS_iSi​ to the next letter in the alphabet.

That is, change A→B,B→C,C→D\text{A} \to \text{B}, \text{B} \to \text{C}, \text{C} \to \text{D}A→B,B→C,C→D, and so on.

The alphabet is considered cyclic, so Z\text{Z}Z can be changed to A\text{A}A.

For example, if SSS equals `"ZARAGOZA"`,


Operating on the first index will turn SSS into `"AARAGOZA"`.
Operating on the second index will instead turn SSS into `"ZBRAGOZA"`.
Operating on the third index will instead turn SSS into `"ZASAGOZA"`.

And so on for the other indices.
Find the minimum number of steps required to convert the string SSS into `"ADVITIYA"`.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT — the number of test cases.
The next TTT lines each contain a string SSS consisting of exactly 888 uppercase English letters.


Output Format

For each test case, output a single integer on a new line — the minimum number of steps to convert SSS into `"ADVITIYA"`.
constraints: Each character of SSS is an uppercase English letter.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: SSS already equals "ADVITIYA", so 000 steps are needed.
Test case 222: "ADVITIAA" can be turned into "ADVITIYA" in 242424 steps, by repeatedly operating on the 777-th character.
That is, "ADVITIAA" becomes "ADVITIBA", then "ADVITICA", then "ADVITIDA", and so on till it reaches "ADVITIYA".
